task_name: Educational Presentation - Not FLS Task Performance

frame_analyses.description: Title slide showing 'Education Short Oral Session' about improving MIS fellowship matching through laparoscopic skills testing
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Academic presentation format with institutional branding

frame_analyses.description: Background slide discussing 28 years of MIS fellowship and skills mismatch challenges
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content about fellowship program challenges

frame_analyses.description: Slide showing structured interview process with SurgWise and MAdSkills testing using FLS Task 1 and 5
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Details about off-axis laparoscopic skills testing implementation

frame_analyses.description: Results slide showing Task 1 performance - 76.9% completion rate at 90° station
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Statistical outcomes of FLS Task 1 testing in fellowship candidates

frame_analyses.description: Task 5 results showing 38.5% failure rate at 0° station, 76.9% failure at 45° off-axis
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates significant difficulty with intracorporeal suturing in off-axis conditions

frame_analyses.description: Conclusions about need for ATLAS training system and faculty retraining
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational recommendations for advanced laparoscopic skills training

confidence_rationale: This is clearly an academic presentation about laparoscopic skills testing in fellowship programs, not an actual FLS task performance video.
technique_summary: Educational presentation discussing implementation of FLS-based testing for MIS fellowship candidate evaluation.

demonstrated_techniques: Implementation of off-axis laparoscopic skills testing
Use of FLS Task 1 (peg transfer) and Task 5 (intracorporeal suturing) for fellowship evaluation
Integration of structured interview processes with technical skills assessment
verbal_cues: Need for objective technical skills assessment
Benefits of off-axis training for real-world surgical scenarios
common_errors_addressed: Relying solely on traditional interview methods without skills assessment
Inadequate preparation of fellowship candidates for advanced MIS procedures
Lack of objective technical skills evaluation in fellowship matching
